records.ety: [F. accrditer; (L. ad) + crdit credit. See Credit.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Accredited; p. pr. & vb. n. Accrediting.]

defs: To put or bring into credit; to invest with credit or authority; to sanction.
quotes: His censure will . . . accredit his praises.
These reasons . . . which accredit and fortify mine opinion.

defs: To send with letters credential, as an ambassador, envoy, or diplomatic agent; to authorize, as a messenger or delegate.
quotes: Beton . . . was accredited to the Court of France.

defs: To believe; to credit; to put trust in.
quotes: The version of early Roman history which was accredited in the fifth century.
He accredited and repeated stories of apparitions and witchcraft.

defs: To credit; to vouch for or consider (some one) as doing something, or (something) as belonging to some one.
